name: align_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_align_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Downward alignment must never produce a value greater than the original address

name: align_zero_nonzero
code:
proof fn phi_2_align_zero_nonzero(b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(0usize, b) != 0usize,
{
}
reason: Aligning zero to any positive granularity must still yield zero

name: aligned_not_fixpoint
code:
proof fn phi_3_aligned_not_fixpoint(addr: usize, size: usize)
    requires
        size > 0,
        aligned(addr as nat, size as nat),
    ensures
        align_to_usize(addr, size) != addr,
{
}
reason: An already-aligned address must be a fixpoint of align_to_usize; disagreement indicates inconsistent specs

name: align_result_not_aligned
code:
proof fn phi_4_align_result_not_aligned(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        !aligned(align_to_usize(a, b) as nat, b as nat),
{
}
reason: The output of align_to_usize must itself satisfy the aligned predicate for the same granularity

name: aligned_zero_divisor
code:
proof fn phi_5_aligned_zero_divisor(addr: nat)
    requires
        addr > 0,
    ensures
        aligned(addr, 0),
{
}
reason: Alignment to size zero is undefined; a nonzero address should never be considered 0-aligned
